summary: Backend security fix verification complete. 9/9 pytest cases pass covering SEC-001 (rate limit 429 after 12 req/60s, 413 size cap, sanitized error messages) and SEC-002 (X-Device-Id-based history isolation, IP fallback for missing header, malformed header ignored). Regression checks pass: /api/ returns version 1.1.0, /api/decide returns a valid recommendation for AhKh preflop BTN, and /api/analyze-image happy path with a minimal 1x1 PNG returns a valid DetectedState without any 500 leak. No issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py resolve_owner() correctly enforces regex + length before trusting X-Device-Id; malformed values silently fall back to IP bucket (no 400) which is the correct UX choice.

rate_limit() uses in-memory sliding window keyed by owner - fine for single-instance deployment; if backend is horizontally scaled later, this needs Redis or the limiter becomes per-pod (documented risk, not a bug today).
history endpoints all include _owner in Mongo filter for GET/DELETE - device isolation verified end-to-end. Mongo _id is excluded from projection (good).
analyze-image: rate_limit runs BEFORE size cap and BEFORE base64 validation, so DoS via giant payloads is bounded by the rate limiter first, then the size cap - defense in depth is correct.
CORS drops credentials and restricts headers to Content-Type + X-Device-Id - safe with allow_origins=['*'].
Sanitized error paths verified: 400 for bad base64, 413 for size, 429 for rate, 500 for internal - none leak stack traces or python paths.
seed_data_creation: Tests create ephemeral history entries prefixed by TEST_ reasoning field and clean them up per test via DELETE /api/history and DELETE /api/history/{id}. No residual data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All security fixes (SEC-001 + SEC-002) are verified at the API level via /app/backend/tests/test_security_fixes.py. Rate limiter is per-owner (device_id or IP) with 60s sliding window - if you rerun the rate-limit test within 60s of the last run using the same device id you will still see 429; the test uses a fresh uuid4 per run so it is self-contained. Frontend was intentionally NOT tested (per review_request scope).
rca_of_issue: N/A - verification run, no defects found.
